category_title: Non-verbal students with moderate to severe cognitive impairments who use modified instrumentation
student_description: Zack is a non-verbal student with moderate to severe cognitive and fine motor impairments who communicates primarily through physical actions and vocalizations. He uniquely requires gross-motor instrument modifications, highly simplified phrase-based musical cues, and an expectation-free environment where his non-verbal contributions are echoed and validated, alongside direct adult support for peer boundaries.

criteria.rationale: Zack lacks the cognitive skills to decode standard notation and gets overwhelmed by playing every note, so he needs simplified, phrase-level anchors to participate musically.
criteria.check: The tutor provides simplified, color-coded, or phrase-based cues rather than expecting Zack to read standard notation or play every note of a song.

criteria.criterion_name: Active Adult Mediation of Social Boundaries
criteria.rationale: Zack ignores peer verbal cues and physical blocking when seeking social engagement, requiring direct adult intervention to manage boundaries.
criteria.check: The tutor directly intervenes to manage Zack's physical boundary-crossing (e.g., hugging, keep-away) rather than relying on peers to enforce verbal boundaries.

criteria.rationale: Because Zack is non-verbal and communicates through movement and vocalizations, echoing his actions validates his communication and builds dialogue.
criteria.check: The tutor acknowledges, echoes, or incorporates Zack's specific vocalizations or physical movements into the activity rather than demanding verbal responses.
